devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति ते वर्णितः क्षत्तः कालाख्यः परमात्मनः । महिमा वेदगर्भोऽथ यथास्राक्षीन्निबोध मे ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaiti te varṇitaḥ kṣattaḥ kālākhyaḥ paramātmanaḥmahimā vedagarbho'tha yathāsrākṣīnnibodha me
translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh Vidura, the greatness of the Kāla-form of the Supreme Lord is thus described to you. Now know from me how Vedagarbha (god Brahmā) proceeded with the creation.

devanagari: ससर्जाग्रेऽन्धतामिस्रं अथ तामिस्रमादिकृत् । महामोहं च मोहं च तमश्चाज्ञानवृत्तयः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sasarjāgre'ndhatāmisraṃ atha tāmisramādikṛtmahāmohaṃ ca mohaṃ ca tamaścājñānavṛttayaḥ
translation: The creator, at first, created the varieties of ignorance (or avidyā) viz. mahāmoha, moha, tāmisra, andhatāmisra and moha[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
For details viḍe supra 3.10.11 and onwards the Sāṅkhya influence is evident. This list is repeated in LP. 2.9.30 and 34-35. This is the tāmasa creation.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा पापीयसीं सृष्टिं नात्मानं बह्वमन्यत । भगवद्ध्यानपूतेन मनसान्यां ततोऽसृजत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā pāpīyasīṃ sṛṣṭiṃ nātmānaṃ bahvamanyatabhagavaddhyānapūtena manasānyāṃ tato'sṛjat
translation: Seeing the sinful creation, Brahmā did not think highly of himself. With his mind purified by meditation on the Lord, he brought forth another creation.

devanagari: सनकं च सनन्दं च सनातनमथात्मभूः । सनत्कुमारं च मुनीन् निष्क्रियान् ऊर्ध्वरेतसः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sanakaṃ ca sanandaṃ ca sanātanamathātmabhūḥsanatkumāraṃ ca munīn niṣkriyān ūrdhvaretasaḥ
translation: The self-born god (Brahmā) then created the sages Sanaka, Sananda, Sanātana and Sanatkumāra who were not interested in actions (for dharma, artha or kāma) and lived in perpetual celibacy[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā adds: These Kumāras are not created in every Kalpa but only in the Brāhma Kalpa. This is the description of the Brāhma Kalpa, hence the mention of their creation.

devanagari: तान् बभाषे स्वभूः पुत्रान् प्रजाः सृजत पुत्रकाः । तन्नैच्छन् मोक्षधर्माणो वासुदेवपरायणाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tān babhāṣe svabhūḥ putrān prajāḥ sṛjata putrakāḥtannaicchan mokṣadharmāṇo vāsudevaparāyaṇāḥ
translation: The self-born god spoke to them, “Oh sons, procreate children”. But they being absolutely devoted to Vāsudeva and followers of the path of liberation, were not so inclined (to create).

devanagari: सोऽवध्यातः सुतैरेवं प्रत्याख्यातानुशासनैः । क्रोधं दुर्विषहं जातं नियन्तुमुपचक्रमे ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: so'vadhyātaḥ sutairevaṃ pratyākhyātānuśāsanaiḥkrodhaṃ durviṣahaṃ jātaṃ niyantumupacakrame
translation: He was thus disobeyed by his sons who refused to comply with his order. He tried to control his unbearable rage aroused (due to this defiance).

devanagari: धिया निगृह्यमाणोऽपि भ्रुवोर्मध्यात्प्रजापतेः । सद्योऽजायत तन्मन्युः कुमारो नीललोहितः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dhiyā nigṛhyamāṇo'pi bhruvormadhyātprajāpateḥsadyo'jāyata tanmanyuḥ kumāro nīlalohitaḥ
translation: Though he tried to control the rage by his mental power, the rage was immediately born as a son of dark-blue complexion from the middle of the brows of Brahmā.

devanagari: स वै रुरोद देवानां पूर्वजो भगवान्भवः । नामानि कुरु मे धातः स्थानानि च जगद्‍गुरो ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai ruroda devānāṃ pūrvajo bhagavānbhavaḥnāmāni kuru me dhātaḥ sthānāni ca jagad‍guro
translation: The illustrious god Bhava who was born before (other) gods verily cried out, “Oh creator, the father of the world, give me names and fix (some) places for me”.

devanagari: इति तस्य वचः पाद्मो भगवान् परिपालयन् । अभ्यधाद् भद्रया वाचा मा रोदीस्तत्करोमि ते ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti tasya vacaḥ pādmo bhagavān paripālayanabhyadhād bhadrayā vācā mā rodīstatkaromi te
translation: The lotus-born god (Brahmā), with a view to granting his request, spoke with sweet words, “Please do not cry, I shall give it to you.

devanagari: यदरोदीः सुरश्रेष्ठ सोद्वेग इव बालकः । ततस्त्वां अभिधास्यन्ति नाम्ना रुद्र इति प्रजाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yadarodīḥ suraśreṣṭha sodvega iva bālakaḥtatastvāṃ abhidhāsyanti nāmnā rudra iti prajāḥ
translation: Oh great god, as you cried like an excited child, people will call you by the name ‘Rudra’.

devanagari: हृदिन्द्रियाण्यसुर्व्योम वायुरग्निर्जलं मही । सूर्यश्चन्द्रस्तपश्चैव स्थानान्यग्रे कृतानि मे ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛdindriyāṇyasurvyoma vāyuragnirjalaṃ mahīsūryaścandrastapaścaiva sthānānyagre kṛtāni me
translation: Your places have been already arranged by me as follows: The mind, the sense-organs, the vital breath, the Sky, the Wind, the Fire, the Water, the Earth, the Sun, the Moon and the penance.

devanagari: मन्युर्मनुर्महिनसो महान् शिव ऋतध्वजः । उग्ररेता भवः कालो वामदेवो धृतव्रतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: manyurmanurmahinaso mahān śiva ṛtadhvajaḥugraretā bhavaḥ kālo vāmadevo dhṛtavrataḥ
translation: (The following are your names): Manyu, Manu, Mahinasa, Mahān, Śiva, Ṛtuḍhvaja, Ugraretas, Bhava, Kāla, Vāmadeva and Dhṛtavrata.

devanagari: धीर्धृतिरसलोमा च नियुत्सर्पिरिलाम्बिका । इरावती सुधा दीक्षा रुद्राण्यो रुद्र ते स्त्रियः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhīrdhṛtirasalomā ca niyutsarpirilāmbikāirāvatī sudhā dīkṣā rudrāṇyo rudra te striyaḥ
translation: Oh Rudra, you will have the following wives, namely: Dhī, Vṛtti, Uśanā, Umā, Niyutsarpi, Ilā, Ambikā, Irāvatī, Sudhā, Dīkṣā, Rudrāṇī.

devanagari: गृहाणैतानि नामानि स्थानानि च सयोषणः । एभिः सृज प्रजा बह्वीः प्रजानामसि यत्पतिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhāṇaitāni nāmāni sthānāni ca sayoṣaṇaḥebhiḥ sṛja prajā bahvīḥ prajānāmasi yatpatiḥ
translation: As you are a Prajāpati accept these names, places along with these wives, and procreate abundant progeny.”

devanagari: इत्यादिष्टः स्वगुरुणा भगवान् नीललोहितः । सत्त्वाकृतिस्वभावेन ससर्जात्मसमाः प्रजाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityādiṣṭaḥ svaguruṇā bhagavān nīlalohitaḥsattvākṛtisvabhāvena sasarjātmasamāḥ prajāḥ
translation: Being thus ordered by his father, Lord Nīlalohita (Rudra) brought forth children who were like him in prowess, form,(complexion) and nature.

devanagari: रुद्राणां रुद्रसृष्टानां समन्ताद् ग्रसतां जगत् । निशाम्यासंख्यशो यूथान् प्रजापतिरशङ्कत ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: rudrāṇāṃ rudrasṛṣṭānāṃ samantād grasatāṃ jagatniśāmyāsaṃkhyaśo yūthān prajāpatiraśaṅkata
translation: Seeing the innumerable hordes of Rudras created by (the original) Rudra, devouring the world on all side, god Brahmā got afraid.

devanagari: अलं प्रजाभिः सृष्टाभिः ईदृशीभिः सुरोत्तम । मया सह दहन्तीभिः दिशश्चक्षुर्भिरुल्बणैः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: alaṃ prajābhiḥ sṛṣṭābhiḥ īdṛśībhiḥ surottamamayā saha dahantībhiḥ diśaścakṣurbhirulbaṇaiḥ
translation: (He said to Rudra): “Oh great god, enough of the creation of all such beings who with their terrible eyes are burning all the directions along with me.

devanagari: तप आतिष्ठ भद्रं ते सर्वभूतसुखावहम् । तपसैव यथापूर्वं स्रष्टा विश्वमिदं भवान् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tapa ātiṣṭha bhadraṃ te sarvabhūtasukhāvahamtapasaiva yathāpūrvaṃ sraṣṭā viśvamidaṃ bhavān
translation: Perform penance which is conducive to the happiness of all beings. May you be blest. It is by penance that you (lit. Your honour) will create again this universe as it was before.

devanagari: तपसैव परं ज्योतिः भगवन्तमधोक्षजम् । सर्वभूतगुहावासं अञ्जसा विन्दते पुमान् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tapasaiva paraṃ jyotiḥ bhagavantamadhokṣajamsarvabhūtaguhāvāsaṃ añjasā vindate pumān
translation: It is only by (performance of) penance that a man can easily attain to the Lord Adhokṣaja, the Supreme light, dwelling in the hearts of all beings.” Maitreya said:

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवमात्मभुवाऽदिष्टः परिक्रम्य गिरां पतिम् । बाढमित्यमुमामन्त्र्य विवेश तपसे वनम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaevamātmabhuvā'diṣṭaḥ parikramya girāṃ patimbāḍhamityamumāmantrya viveśa tapase vanam
translation: Being thus ordered by the self-born god (Brahmā), (Rudra) circumambulated from left to right the Lord of Speech (Brahmā). Complying with his request, he bade goodbye to him and entered the forest for penance.

devanagari: अथाभिध्यायतः सर्गं दश पुत्राः प्रजज्ञिरे । भगवत् शक्तियुक्तस्य लोकसन्तानहेतवः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: athābhidhyāyataḥ sargaṃ daśa putrāḥ prajajñirebhagavat śaktiyuktasya lokasantānahetavaḥ
translation: Then while he was musing over the problem of creation and became possessed of the power of the Lord (for creation), ten sons who became the progenitors of the population of the world, were born to him.

devanagari: मरीचिरत्र्यङ्गिरसौ पुलस्त्यः पुलहः क्रतुः । भृगुर्वसिष्ठो दक्षश्च दशमस्तत्र नारदः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: marīciratryaṅgirasau pulastyaḥ pulahaḥ kratuḥbhṛgurvasiṣṭho dakṣaśca daśamastatra nāradaḥ
translation: [3] (They were): Marīci, Atri, Aṅgiras, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Dakṣa and the tenth (son) was Nārada.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
For the symbolism of the ten mind-born sons as Virāj vide V.S. Agrawal—MP.—A Study, p. 38.

devanagari: उत्सङ्गान्नारदो जज्ञे दक्षोऽङ्गुष्ठात्स्वयम्भुवः । प्राणाद्वसिष्ठः सञ्जातो भृगुस्त्वचि करात्क्रतुः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: utsaṅgānnārado jajñe dakṣo'ṅguṣṭhātsvayambhuvaḥprāṇādvasiṣṭhaḥ sañjāto bhṛgustvaci karātkratuḥ
translation: Nārada was born from Brahmā’s lap; Dakṣa from his thumb; Vasiṣṭha from his vital breath, Bhṛgu from the skin and Kratu from his hand; Pulaha was born from his navel, the sage Pulastya from his ears, Aṅgiras from his mouth, Atri from his eyes, and Marīci, was created from (Brahmā’s) mind.

devanagari: पुलहो नाभितो जज्ञे पुलस्त्यः कर्णयोः ऋषिः । अङ्गिरा मुखतोऽक्ष्णोऽत्रिः मरीचिर्मनसोऽभवत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: pulaho nābhito jajñe pulastyaḥ karṇayoḥ ṛṣiḥaṅgirā mukhato'kṣṇo'triḥ marīcirmanaso'bhavat
translation: Nārada was born from Brahmā’s lap; Dakṣa from his thumb; Vasiṣṭha from his vital breath, Bhṛgu from the skin and Kratu from his hand; Pulaha was born from his navel, the sage Pulastya from his ears, Aṅgiras from his mouth, Atri from his eyes, and Marīci, was created from (Brahmā’s) mind.

devanagari: धर्मः स्तनाद् दक्षिणतो यत्र नारायणः स्वयम् । अधर्मः पृष्ठतो यस्मात् मृत्युर्लोकभयङ्करः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmaḥ stanād dakṣiṇato yatra nārāyaṇaḥ svayamadharmaḥ pṛṣṭhato yasmāt mṛtyurlokabhayaṅkaraḥ
translation: Dharma was born from his right breast wherein abides Nārāyaṇa himself. From his back (was born) Adharma which begets the Death, a terror to the world.

devanagari: हृदि कामो भ्रुवः क्रोधो लोभश्चाधरदच्छदात् । आस्याद् वाक्सिन्धवो मेढ्रान् निर्ऋतिः पायोरघाश्रयः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛdi kāmo bhruvaḥ krodho lobhaścādharadacchadātāsyād vāksindhavo meḍhrān nirṛtiḥ pāyoraghāśrayaḥ
translation: Desire (Kāma) was born in his heart; Anger (krodha) from his brow; Avarice (Lobha) from his lower lip; Speech was born from (his) mouth; the rivers from his penis and Nirṛti, the shelter of all sins, from his anus.

devanagari: छायायाः कर्दमो जज्ञे देवहूत्याः पतिः प्रभुः । मनसो देहतश्चेदं जज्ञे विश्वकृतो जगत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: chāyāyāḥ kardamo jajñe devahūtyāḥ patiḥ prabhuḥmanaso dehataścedaṃ jajñe viśvakṛto jagat
translation: (The powerful sage) Kardama, the husband of Devahūti, was born from his shadow; this (whole) world was created from the mind and the body of the creator of the universe.

devanagari: वाचं दुहितरं तन्वीं स्वयम्भूर्हरतीं मनः । अकामां चकमे क्षत्तः सकाम इति नः श्रुतम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vācaṃ duhitaraṃ tanvīṃ svayambhūrharatīṃ manaḥakāmāṃ cakame kṣattaḥ sakāma iti naḥ śrutam
translation: Oh Vidura, it is reported to us that self-born god became passionate and desired his own beautiful daughter, the goddess of Speech who, though herself above passions, captivated his mind.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Cf. The ‘marriage’ of Brahmā and Śatarūpā in MP. and the explanation of this symbolism vide V.S. Agrawal—MP.—A Study, pp. 47-50.
Padaratnāvalī however maintains that vāc is ‘the deity presiding over human speech’ and not Sarasvatī who is the natural consort of Brahmā, the Veda incarnate.

devanagari: तमधर्मे कृतमतिं विलोक्य पितरं सुताः । मरीचिमुख्या मुनयो विश्रम्भात् प्रत्यबोधयन् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tamadharme kṛtamatiṃ vilokya pitaraṃ sutāḥmarīcimukhyā munayo viśrambhāt pratyabodhayan
translation: His sons, the sages, of whom Marīci was the foremost, saw that their father had set his heart on unrighteousness. Out of familiar confidence, they pleaded with (lit. awakened) him.

devanagari: नैतत्पूर्वैः कृतं त्वद्ये न करिष्यन्ति चापरे । यस्त्वं दुहितरं गच्छेः अनिगृह्याङ्गजं प्रभुः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: naitatpūrvaiḥ kṛtaṃ tvadye na kariṣyanti cāpareyastvaṃ duhitaraṃ gaccheḥ anigṛhyāṅgajaṃ prabhuḥ
translation: “That you do not control your passion despite your capacity to do so, and that you are bent on approaching your daughter, has never been done by the ancestors nor will it be done by others after you in future.

devanagari: तेजीयसामपि ह्येतन्न सुश्लोक्यं जगद्‍गुरो । यद्वृत्तमनुतिष्ठन् वै लोकः क्षेमाय कल्पते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tejīyasāmapi hyetanna suślokyaṃ jagad‍guroyadvṛttamanutiṣṭhan vai lokaḥ kṣemāya kalpate
translation: Oh father of the world, this is not glorifying (creditable) even to the possessors of divine lustre (power). By following this (course of conduct) the people shall never attain any good.”

devanagari: तस्मै नमो भगवते य इदं स्वेन रोचिषा । आत्मस्थं व्यञ्जयामास स धर्मं पातुमर्हति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai namo bhagavate ya idaṃ svena rociṣāātmasthaṃ vyañjayāmāsa sa dharmaṃ pātumarhati
translation: (Finding Brahmā bent on the heinous act the sages prayed to God) “We bow to the glorious Lord who manifested this world that was within him. He alone is capable of protecting righteousness.”

devanagari: स इत्थं गृणतः पुत्रान् पुरो दृष्ट्वा प्रजापतीन् । प्रजापतिपतिस्तन्वं तत्याज व्रीडितस्तदा । तां दिशो जगृहुर्घोरां नीहारं यद्विदुस्तमः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: sa itthaṃ gṛṇataḥ putrān puro dṛṣṭvā prajāpatīnprajāpatipatistanvaṃ tatyāja vrīḍitastadātāṃ diśo jagṛhurghorāṃ nīhāraṃ yadvidustamaḥ
translation: Then the father of the Prajāpatis, seeing his sons, the Prajāpatis speaking this in his presence, became ashamed and gave up his body.

devanagari: कदाचिद् ध्यायतः स्रष्टुः वेदा आसंश्चतुर्मुखात् । कथं स्रक्ष्याम्यहं लोकान् समवेतान् यथा पुरा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kadācid dhyāyataḥ sraṣṭuḥ vedā āsaṃścaturmukhātkathaṃ srakṣyāmyahaṃ lokān samavetān yathā purā
translation: The cardinal points accepted that terrible body. The wise people know it as mist or darkness. While he was meditating how he should create the well-planned worlds as before, the Vedas came out from the four mouths of the creator.

devanagari: चातुर्होत्रं कर्मतन्त्रं उपवेदनयैः सह । धर्मस्य पादाश्चत्वारः तथैवाश्रमवृत्तयः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: cāturhotraṃ karmatantraṃ upavedanayaiḥ sahadharmasya pādāścatvāraḥ tathaivāśramavṛttayaḥ
translation: The duties of the four sacrificial priests[5] (hotā, adhvaryu, udgātā, brahmā) along with the upavedas and the disciplines of Logic etc., the extensive course of sacrificial sessions, the four legs of Dharma and the duties of the four Āśramas were also produced thence.”
purport:
Footnote [5]:
These Priests are the experts in the Ṛg and other Vedas seri ally.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । स वै विश्वसृजामीशो वेदादीन् मुखतोऽसृजत् । यद् यद् येनासृजद् देवस्तन्मे ब्रूहि तपोधन ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvācasa vai viśvasṛjāmīśo vedādīn mukhato'sṛjatyad yad yenāsṛjad devastanme brūhi tapodhana
translation:
Vidura said:
“Oh sage (lit. one who regards penance as his wealth) you told that the Lord of the progenitors of the world produced Vedas etc. from his mouths. Please tell me by what particular (organ) he produced the specific things.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । ऋग्यजुःसामाथर्वाख्यान् वेदान् पूर्वादिभिर्मुखैः । शास्त्रमिज्यां स्तुतिस्तोमं प्रायश्चित्तं व्यधात्क्रमात् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaṛgyajuḥsāmātharvākhyān vedān pūrvādibhirmukhaiḥśāstramijyāṃ stutistomaṃ prāyaścittaṃ vyadhātkramāt
translation:
Maitreya said:
“From his mouths facing the east (the west etc.), he produced the Vedas—Ṛg, Yajus, Sāman and Atharvan and Śastra (simple recitation of mantras in praise of gods entrusted to ṛtvij), ijyā (oblations, worship etc. to be done by adhvaryu), stutistoma (singing of the mantras in praise of gods, the duty of udgātā) and Prāyaścitta (expiatory rites in case of some lapses in sacrifices to be performed by brahmā).

devanagari: आयुर्वेदं धनुर्वेदं गान्धर्वं वेदमात्मनः । स्थापत्यं चासृजद् वेदं क्रमात् पूर्वादिभिर्मुखैः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: āyurvedaṃ dhanurvedaṃ gāndharvaṃ vedamātmanaḥsthāpatyaṃ cāsṛjad vedaṃ kramāt pūrvādibhirmukhaiḥ
translation: Again through the same faces, and in the same serial order, he produced Āyurveda (the science of medicine), Dhanurveda (science of Warfare), Gandharva Veda (the science of Music) and Sthāpatya Veda (the science of Architecture, Sculpture etc.).

devanagari: इतिहासपुराणानि पञ्चमं वेदमीश्वरः । सर्वेभ्य एव वक्त्रेभ्यः ससृजे सर्वदर्शनः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: itihāsapurāṇāni pañcamaṃ vedamīśvaraḥsarvebhya eva vaktrebhyaḥ sasṛje sarvadarśanaḥ
translation: The Omniscient Lord produced the fifth Veda, viz. Itihāsa and Purāṇas from all his mouths.

devanagari: षोडश्युक्थौ पूर्ववक्त्रात् पुरीष्यग्निष्टुतावथ । आप्तोर्यामातिरात्रौ च वाजपेयं सगोसवम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ṣoḍaśyukthau pūrvavaktrāt purīṣyagniṣṭutāvathaāptoryāmātirātrau ca vājapeyaṃ sagosavam
translation: From his mouth facing the east (came forth the sacrificial acts viz.) Ṣoḍaṣī and Uktha (and serially from other mouths) Purīṣī and Agniṣṭut, Āptoryāma and Aiirātra, Vājapeya, along with Gosava.

devanagari: विद्या दानं तपः सत्यं धर्मस्येति पदानि च । आश्रमांश्च यथासंख्यं असृजत्सह वृत्तिभिः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyā dānaṃ tapaḥ satyaṃ dharmasyeti padāni caāśramāṃśca yathāsaṃkhyaṃ asṛjatsaha vṛttibhiḥ
translation: He created the four feet of Dharma viz. Vidyā (knowledge and purity), dāna (donations), tapas (penance) and satya (Truth). He also produced the (lour) āśramas (stages in life) along with their types of duties.

devanagari: सावित्रं प्राजापत्यं च ब्राह्मं चाथ बृहत्तथा । वार्ता सञ्चयशालीन शिलोञ्छ इति वै गृहे ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sāvitraṃ prājāpatyaṃ ca brāhmaṃ cātha bṛhattathāvārtā sañcayaśālīna śiloñcha iti vai gṛhe
translation: (Brahmacarya āśrama)—celibate stage—the 1st stage in life—is of four types viz.) Sāvitra (observance of celibacy and study of Sāvitrī for three nights from the upanayana (threadbearing) ceremony), Prajāpatya (observance of celibacy for one year), brāhma (observance of celibacy till the completion of the study of Vedas) and bṛhat (celibacy throughout life). (The 2nd stage in life) The gṛhastha āśrama—the married stage—is of four kinds according to the way of maintenance of family[6]: Vārtā (earning livelihood by agriculture and such other non-prohibited vocations), Sañcaya (maintenance of money earned by performing sacrifices), Śālīna (to live on whatever one gets without requesting or begging), Śiloñccha—(maintenance by gleaning grains, grain-gathering).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Vaikhānasa Gṛhya Sūtra classifies gṛhasthas as (1) Vārtā vṛtti (maintaining on agriculture, trade), (2) Śālina vṛtti (stay in houses performing sacrifices etc.)., (3) Yāyāvara (performing ṣaṭkarmas) (4) Ghoracārika—corresponding to Śiloñccha. For details vide Kane—History of Dharma Śāstra, Vol. II. I. pp. 641-43.

devanagari: वैखानसा वालखिल्यौ दुम्बराः फेनपा वने । न्यासे कुटीचकः पूर्वं बह्वोदो हंसनिष्क्रियौ ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: vaikhānasā vālakhilyau dumbarāḥ phenapā vanenyāse kuṭīcakaḥ pūrvaṃ bahvodo haṃsaniṣkriyau
translation: In (the third stage of life called) the vānaprastha āśrama, (there are four categories viz.) Vaikhānasas (who live upon food-grains which grow naturally without agricultural efforts), Vālakhilyas (who subsist on fresh food, giving away hoarded food), Audumbaras (who eat fruits etc. gathered from the direction to which they happen to look at first in the morning) and Phenapa (who live on fruits, leaves etc. automatically dropped from trees). In (the last stage of life) the Saṃnyāsa āśrama, (the four kinds are:) Kuṭīcaka (who chiefly abide by all the duties prescribed for the āśrama), Bahvoda (who perform the most essential duties of this āśrama but emphasize on the path of knowledge), Haṃsa (who concentrate on knowledge), Niṣkriya (who have realized the Soul).[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Padaratnāvalī and Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa differ: (1) Vaikhānasas—Those who subsist on roots, (ii) Vālakhilyas—Who eat everything, (iii) Udumbaras—Who live upon fruits, (iv) Phenapas—Who subsist on the milk-foam falling out from, the mouth of calves while sucking.
This classification is supported by Bṛhat Parāśara Smṛti XII. 158 for Vānaprasthas and XII.164 for Saṃnyāsins.
Kinds of Saṃnyāsins:—
Kuṭīcaka:
(i) The Ascetic who lives in his own house and is satisfied on whatever food is given to him—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) The ascetic who due to weakness etc. does not wander from one holy place to another but stays at one holy place in a hut and lives upon begging after practising japa of 12000 Praṇavas—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa
Bahvoda:
(i) He who, in addition to his three baths per day, performs ablution in every new tīrtha he sets and has three daṇḍas—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Who wanders from one holy place to another not living beyond a prescribed period at each place (e.g. I night at a village, 3 nights in a city etc.)
Haṃsa:
(i) Who carries one daṇḍa and the sacred thread—Yajñopavīta—Padaratnāvalī (ii) Who is nude, beyond dvandvas, lost in deep meditation of Brahman—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa
Niṣkrīya or Parama-haṃsa—
(i) Who has abandoned all acts, completely observing ahiṃsā.
(ii) One who is absorbed in the meditation of the Brahman, careless about his body etc. and has crossed the seven stages of knowledge (Sapta- Jñāna-Bhūmikās).
Bhāgavata Candrikā and VC. follow Bhāvāratha Dīpikā Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa claims his acceptance of Bhāvāratha Dīpikā’s interpretation.

devanagari: आन्वीक्षिकी त्रयी वार्ता दण्डनीतिस्तथैव च । एवं व्याहृतयश्चासन् प्रणवो ह्यस्य दह्रतः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: ānvīkṣikī trayī vārtā daṇḍanītistathaiva caevaṃ vyāhṛtayaścāsan praṇavo hyasya dahrataḥ
translation: (So also were serially produced) the science of the knowledge of Soul and non-Soul[8], the three Vedas, the science of agriculture and the science of politics (leading to the attainment of Mokṣa, dharma, kāma and artha) and also the vyāhṛtis (mystic words following Om or praṇava) viz. bhūḥ, bhuvaḥ, suvaḥ and bhūr-bhuvaḥ-suvaḥ (or mahas). From his heart came forth Om.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
āṇvīkṣikī—The Tantrism in consonance with the Vedas—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: १ तस्योष्णिगासील्लोमभ्यो गायत्री च त्वचो विभोः । २ त्रिष्टुम्मांसात्स्नुतोऽनुष्टुब्३ ४ जगत्यस्थ्नः५ प्रजापतेः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: 1 tasyoṣṇigāsīllomabhyo gāyatrī ca tvaco vibhoḥ2 triṣṭummāṃsātsnuto'nuṣṭub3 4 jagatyasthnaḥ5 prajāpateḥ
translation: (The Vedic metres) Uṣṇik, Gāyatrī, Triṣṭubh, Anuṣṭubh and Jagatī, Paṅkti and Bṛhatī were produced respectively from the hair, skin, flesh, muscles and bones, marrow and prāṇa or vital airs of the all-pervading Prajāpati (Brahmā). His life (jīva) is said to consist of the occlusives or the consonants of five classes (e.g. velar, palatal etc.) from k to m, and his body, of vowels (a, i, u etc.)[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Padaratnāvalī states: The occlusives from k to m were produced from his jīva and the vowels from his body.

devanagari: मज्जायाः पङ्क्तिरुत्पन्ना बृहती प्राणतोऽभवत् । स्पर्शस्तस्याभवज्जीवः स्वरो देह उदाहृत ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: majjāyāḥ paṅktirutpannā bṛhatī prāṇato'bhavatsparśastasyābhavajjīvaḥ svaro deha udāhṛta
translation: (The Vedic metres) Uṣṇik, Gāyatrī, Triṣṭubh, Anuṣṭubh and Jagatī, Paṅkti and Bṛhatī were produced respectively from the hair, skin, flesh, muscles and bones, marrow and prāṇa or vital airs of the all-pervading Prajāpati (Brahmā). His life (jīva) is said to consist of the occlusives or the consonants of five classes (e.g. velar, palatal etc.) from k to m, and his body, of vowels (a, i, u etc.)[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Padaratnāvalī states: The occlusives from k to m were produced from his jīva and the vowels from his body.
